title: Print Pattern

content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN, you have to print an upside down triangle with stars of base NNN.

For example: for N=5N = 5N=5:

`*****
****
***
**
*
`
Input Format

You are given a single integer in input NNN.


Output Format
You have to output a the pattern specified above based on the integer NNN.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N, the width of the base of the upside‑down triangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print N lines. The i‑th line contains N‑i+1 asterisks '*'. Separate consecutive test cases by a blank line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, N=4. The triangle starts with 4 stars and decreases by one each line.
Two test cases. First prints a triangle of size 3, then a blank line, then a triangle of size 5.
T=1, N=2. The smallest non‑trivial case prints two stars then one star.

solution.approach: The required pattern is straightforward: for each test case we start with N stars and decrease the count by one after each line until we reach one star. We simply iterate i from N down to 1 and output i asterisks. A blank line is printed between test cases (except after the last one). This runs in O(N^2) time per test case because the total number of printed characters is N+(N-1)+...+1 = N(N+1)/2, and uses O(1) auxiliary space besides the output buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case (total characters printed), O(T * N^2) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (output stored in a StringBuilder).
